prompt: A dense isometric steampunk-cyberpunk city illustration at dusk, designed like a whimsical indie tech district called INDIE MIND CITY. The scene is a single sprawling urban plaza packed with narrow brick buildings, rooftop antennas, cables, pipes, neon signs, market stalls, tiny pedestrians, and playful programmer humor. In the center background, a plaza labeled “PLAZA DEL PROMPT” surrounds a sculptural fountain with pale blue water, with benches and people gathered around it. To the left background, a tall building with a giant sign reading “BARRIO CÓDIGO ABIERTO” is covered in balconies, windows, hanging laundry, air conditioners, plants, and street activity. To the right background, another block bears a large sign “CALLEJÓN DE LOS AGENTES,” with stacked storefront signs including “Codex Barbería cortes exactos,” “Gemini Lavandería,” “Claude’s Coffee,” and “Dr. Llama.” Further right, a heavy industrial factory zone with smokestacks, cranes, ducts, crates, and catwalks displays 3 large signs: “ZONA INDUSTRIAL,” “FÁBRICA DE TOKENS,” and “LLM.” A cloud of floating letters and symbols rises above the chimneys like generated text smoke. In the foreground center, a tall clocktower-like corner building has a vertical neon sign reading “INDIE MIND,” a large round clock on the facade, warm lit windows, and a small stone animal sculpture at its base. To the right foreground, a lively market district glows with string lights and a bright neon sign “MERCADO NOCTURNO,” with 3 visible stall signs: “Memoria usada barata,” “Embeddings frescos del día,” and “Se cambian vectores.” Nearby are 3 more joke signs: “HARD HAT: prompt injection zone,” “HACKATHON: entra y sal con un bug,” and a food stand labeled “STACK OVERFLOW NOODLES.” To the left foreground, an older mixed-use building is filled with signs and visual jokes, including a huge rooftop billboard with 2 cats on top and the title INDIE MIND CITY, plus a small version label “v1.0.0.” On that same block, include these visible signs: “FORGE & CAFE,” “git push cafe +$2,” “Tienda de MERGE CONFLICTS abierto 24/7,” “Se reparan linters,” “NO rm -rf aquí,” “Ralph was here,” “npm install vv,” “sudo make me sandwich,” “WORKTREE,” “SE VENDE GPU USADA,” “MUSEO DEL MERGE CONFLICT,” “NOTARÍA DE COMMITS FIRMADOS,” “PSICÓLOGO PARA SENIOR DEVS 50m 80,” “GATO PERDIDO responde a localhost,” and a poster board reading “HOY: charla sobre agentic coding entrada libre” and “REUNIÓN DE AA^AI REUNIDOS DE LOS AGI.” Near the center-left plaza, add a sandwich-board style sign labeled “PROMPTS USADOS” with 3 lines beneath it, and small plaza signs that read “HELLO WORLD,” “Tailscale,” “OpenRouter,” and “Deepseek.” The streets are cobblestone and crowded with exact small story details: 1 yellow tram labeled “LÍNEA MAIN,” 1 small blue car, 1 bicycle cart, 1 delivery scooter, outdoor café tables, chess players, pedestrians, crates, potted plants, striped awnings, laundry lines, glowing windows, rooftop cats, and a white llama standing near the agent alley. Use a warm muted palette of dusty teal, rust, amber, tan, and faded neon pink and cyan. Render it as an ultra-detailed editorial illustration with clean isometric perspective, intricate linework, soft atmospheric lighting, tiny readable signage, cozy urban clutter, and humorous handcrafted worldbuilding.
